title: Route Windows .cmd shims through cmd.exe with manual quoting
context: Node >=18.20's CVE-2024-27980 hardening refuses to spawn .cmd shims without shell. npm is a .cmd on Windows; spawnSync(executable, args) fails ENOENT, breaking every local gate run.
decision: Detect Windows + non-node commands; spawn through cmd.exe with manually-quoted args (winQuote function). Keep shell-free argv for node. Avoids shell:true (DEP0190 deprecated, concatenates args unescaped).
consequences: npm runs work on Windows (fixes gate ENOENT)
All non-node Windows commands routed through cmd.exe
Per-argument quoting prevents shell metachar injection
Platform-specific code branch added (win32 vs others)
Node preserves shell-free execution (retains CVE hardening)
alternatives_rejected: shell:true — simpler but DEP0190 deprecated; concatenates args unescaped (security risk)
Keep shell-free spawnSync everywhere — fails ENOENT on Windows .cmd shims

rejected_tripwires.alternative: shell:true — simpler but DEP0190 deprecated; concatenates args unescaped (security risk)
Keep shell-free spawnSync everywhere — fails ENOENT on Windows .cmd shims
